item.title: Which tokens does a hybrid model predict better?
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face Blog has a new AI-related update around 'Which tokens does a hybrid model predict better?', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: How agents are transforming work
item.description: Focus: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

item.title: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face Blog has a new AI-related update around 'Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Focus: Move over, Harness Engineering, it is time for the harness of harnesses!

item.title: Teaching agents product design at Vercel
item.description: Focus: Coding agents can produce working UI fast, but what's harder is a different shape. They can copy your product's style, match its patterns, and try to follow its conventions. What they cannot do is understand why those patterns exist. Code shows agents what shipped, not why one component, phra

item.title: The Ultimate Summer Sale Pairing: Steam Sale Meets GeForce NOW Discounts
item.description: Focus: Summer savings are heating up. From the Steam Summer Sale to GeForce NOW membership discounts, this week’s GFN Thursday delivers double the deals and more ways to get the most value from cloud gaming. Plus, Dark Scrolls joins the growing Devolver lineup, alongside Square Enix’s The Adventures

item.title: AIUC-1: Building trust in AI agents
item.description: Focus: How do we build trust in AI agents before the AI hailstorm arrives? Emil Lassen from the Artificial Intelligence Underwriting Company (AIUC) joins the show to discuss how the enterprise flywheel of standards, certification, audit, and insurance is being applied to AI agents. They explore the

item.title: AI SDK 7
item.description: Focus: AI SDK, with over 16 million weekly downloads, is the TypeScript SDK for building AI applications, features, frameworks, and agents across any model provider. It's the same layer eve , Vercel's open-source agent framework, is built on. AI SDK 7 adds production depth for agent work across five

item.title: AI SDK 7 is now available
item.description: Focus: AI SDK 7 is a major release for building production agents in TypeScript. The SDK has grown from model calls and chat primitives into a broader agent platform for developing, running, integrating, and observing agents across text, audio, realtime, image, and video. Every major provider is sup

item.title: Deep Agents and OpenCode are now available in the AI SDK Harness
item.description: Focus: The AI SDK Harness lets you run established coding-agent runtimes through one unified interface, so you can switch runtimes without changing your application code. Today we're adding two new adapters, Deep Agents and OpenCode, both running inside a Vercel Sandbox. Deep Agents @ai-sdk/harness-

item.title: Vercel Flags no longer requires SDK Keys for Vercel deployments
item.description: Focus: New projects using Vercel Flags no longer need to configure SDK Keys or the FLAGS environment variable when evaluating flags inside a Vercel deployment. At runtime, the Vercel adapter automatically receives a short-lived OIDC token, so authentication is handled for you with zero configuration

item.title: Pro teams can now run up to 500 concurrent builds
item.description: Focus: Pro teams can run up to 500 concurrent builds, up from 12. Teams with large repos can ship faster without waiting on queued builds. On-demand concurrent builds are enabled by default for Pro and Enterprise, and you're billed only for the build minutes you use. Learn more about on-demand concu

item.title: Understanding the brain with AI-driven explanations and experiments
item.description: Focus: Researchers introduce generative causal testing, which translates black box models into clear hypotheses and verifies them in the scanner, revealing what specific brain regions respond to in language. The post Understanding the brain with AI-driven explanations and experiments appeared first
